title: orchis cordigera subsp. blyttii
intro: Orchis cordigera subsp. blyttii, also known as Blytt's orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of orchid native to Norway. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as gout, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Blytt's orchid include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared in the form of an infusion or tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments.
